findings.description: The output overstates the poverty of the packet by saying it only gives metadata and high-level notes, while later relying on specific packet notes about GPT-3 zero/one/few-shot settings, MMLU defined settings, and contamination discussion.
findings.evidence: Gold expects capture of claimed capability/benchmark evidence, prompt protocol, and source-reported contamination analysis; B0 sometimes frames these as mostly unavailable rather than as limited but present source summaries.

findings.description: Traceability is mostly by generic packet references rather than explicit evidence labels, pages, sections, or source-location caveats.
findings.evidence: Gold values separating source facts from critique and reporting source/page/table grounding where available; B0 does not use evidence labels or cite locations beyond broad references to GPT-3 and MMLU notes.

scorecards.summary: B0 is a strong closed-source rigor audit. It captures benchmark scores as protocol-bound, flags contamination, prompt underspecification, baseline weakness, statistics, leakage, and claim narrowing. Its main weakness is somewhat generic grounding and occasional overstatement that the packet lacks details it later acknowledges.
scorecards.scoring_notes: No hard fail. It avoids external facts and does not invent numeric results. Scores are strong but below excellent because source/evidence boundaries are less explicit than the best variants.

findings.description: The output uses packet evidence columns and support-status labels, but still lacks exact page/table/section grounding and does not explicitly distinguish source-reported contamination analysis from all follow-up risks in every instance.
findings.evidence: Gold M3 asks for separation of source-reported contamination analysis from additional contamination risks; B1 does this reasonably but not with the explicit source/critique labeling seen in B3.

findings.description: Benchmark saturation is described as plausible from public/many-task use but remains somewhat inferred; the answer correctly marks it unquantified, limiting concern.
findings.evidence: Gold permits contamination and saturation skepticism but requires not overstating beyond packet evidence.

scorecards.summary: B1 is concise, well calibrated, and covers all required audit components. It is especially efficient and usable, with clear tables for risks, baselines, claims, and corrected protocol. It avoids external evidence and broad claims.
scorecards.scoring_notes: No hard fail. Strong across dimensions. Slightly less complete than B3 because evidence labels and inference boundaries are present but not as explicit or systematic.

findings.description: Some recommended checks mention benchmark release dates and model training data cutoff. As corrective protocol this is appropriate, but it should be clearly framed as required metadata rather than implying the packet contains or establishes those facts.
findings.evidence: Gold requires using only packet evidence and separating proposed follow-up checks from source facts.

findings.description: The audit is detailed and well structured, but table density makes it slightly less direct than B1/B3 for a review artifact.
findings.evidence: Required outputs are all present, but the format has some repetition across findings, risk table, and baseline review.

scorecards.summary: B2 is a strong audit that captures the central benchmark-contamination skepticism and gives a solid corrected protocol. It handles baselines, metrics, uncertainty, prompt protocol, leakage, and claim scope well. It remains within the closed-source boundary.
scorecards.scoring_notes: No hard fail. Comparable to B1, slightly less efficient. It is scientifically reliable with only minor boundary-format concerns.

findings.description: The output identifies baseline comparability needs but cannot deeply normalize comparisons because the packet omits exact baselines and metrics. This is a limitation of the task materials rather than a serious output flaw.
findings.evidence: Gold notes ambiguities requiring human review for exact page, section, table, and figure locations, and expects baseline selection and metric appropriateness to be audited from the packet.

findings.description: The explicit evidence-burden labels improve rigor but add some verbosity compared with B1. The added structure is useful, so this is only a minor efficiency cost.
findings.evidence: The output repeats DIRECT_PACKET_EVIDENCE, LIMITED_CRITIQUE, and UNSUPPORTED_OR_NEEDS_EXTERNAL_REVIEW labels across sections.

scorecards.summary: B3 is the best calibrated variant. It explicitly inventories supplied sources, separates direct packet evidence, within-packet synthesis, limited critique, and unsupported claims, and fully covers contamination, saturation, prompt protocol, baselines, uncertainty, leakage, and claim scope. It avoids external facts and does not invent numbers.
scorecards.scoring_notes: No hard fail. This most closely matches the gold requirements, especially M1-M3 and the inference-boundary instructions. Minor limits are due to missing source details in the packet, not output error.
